channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, KTLA 5

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck vandals caught on video scrawling 'Nazis' and swastika: NYPD
item.description: The New York Police Department Hate Crime Task Force is investigating after two men were caught on camera carving "Nazis" and a swastika onto a Cybertruck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: A federal judge has halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the Voice of America, the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, pushing past opposition from dentists and national health organizations who warn the move will lead to medical problems that disproportionately affect low-income communities. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: Investigators at the medical examiner's office do not suspect any foul play, a representative told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles issues only 4 permits to rebuild homes after devastating Palisades fire: report
item.description: Only four permits to rebuild damaged or destroyed homes in the Pacific Palisades area of Los Angeles have been issued, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury from outside of Memphis will hear the case of 3 former officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A jury from outside of Memphis will be seated in the state court trial of three former police officers charged with second-degree murder in the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023, a judge ruled Friday. Shelby County Judge James Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Joe Harris, believed to be the oldest surviving WWII paratrooper, has died
item.description: Sgt. Joe Harris, believed to be the oldest surviving World War II paratrooper and a member of the U.S. Army’s first all-Black parachute infantry battalion, has died. He was 108. Harris died March 15 in a hospital in Los Angeles surrounded by family, grandson Ashton Pittman told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boy, 14, dies from apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound in California
item.description: IRVINE (KTLA) — A 14-year-old boy in Irvine has died from an apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound that has resulted in an adult facing criminal charges. Officers and emergency medical personnel responded to a home in the area of Heathergreen and Weepingwood around 2:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: David Childs, architect of One World Trade Center that rose on Twin Towers site after 9/11, dies
item.description: David Childs, the lead architect of the One World Trade Center skyscraper that rose from the site where the Twin Towers collapsed in New York City during the 9/11 attacks, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: A second South Carolina death row inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina death row inmate on Friday chose execution by firing squad, just five weeks after the state carried out its first death by bullets. Mikal Mahdi, who pleaded guilty to murder for killing a police officer in 2004, is scheduled to be executed April 11.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tufts University student arrest: Top Dems want answers from Noem, Rubio, ICE on 'deeply troubling' incident
item.description: Top Democrats are calling for the release of Tufts University graduate student Rumeysa Ozturk, writing a letter to Trump administration officials demanding answers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A look at who has been detained or deported in a US crackdown on mostly pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: People with ties to American universities, most of whom have shown support for pro-Palestinian causes, have been detained in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Organ transplant patient in Michigan dies from rabies
item.description: A Michigan resident died earlier this year after contracting rabies from an organ transplant, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Caregiver was at nail salon when autistic woman went missing: Police
item.description: The caregiver initially told investigators DaLomba vanished while she was using the bathroom at the Burlington Coat Factory.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Bestselling author of 'The Vampire Diaries' dies at 66
item.description: Bestselling author L.J. Smith, best known for the book series "The Vampire Diaries," which also became a TV hit, has died at 66.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: 30 years after music icon Selena's murder, Yolanda Saldívar is up for parole. Here's what to know
item.description: Thirty years ago, music legend Selena Quintanilla-Pérez was killed by her fan club’s president, Yolanda Saldívar. For the last three decades, Saldívar has served her life sentence in Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN AP Music Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Atlantic publishes Trump Cabinet group chat messages
item.description: The Atlantic has published the Signal group chat messages among national security leaders that were inadvertently shared with Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg, noting administration officials said Tuesday they were not classified.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: LDS church applies for smaller temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching agreement; new rendering released
item.description: The LDS church says it has filed a new application for a controversial temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching an agreement with the town.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
